description: Full mapping of all 11 BCBS 239 principles to platform capabilities

principles.description: A bank's board and senior management should establish strong governance arrangements for risk data aggregation and risk reporting

evidence_links.description: 4 roles (analyst, compliance_officer, data_engineer, admin) with tier_access, classification_access
Cross-view masking enforcement — consistent governance across all data endpoints

principles.description: A bank should design, build and maintain data architecture and IT infrastructure which fully supports its risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices
A bank should be able to generate accurate risk data to meet normal and stress/crisis reporting accuracy requirements

evidence_links.description: Quality scoring with thresholds (critical <60, warning 60-80, healthy >80)

principles.description: A bank should be able to capture and aggregate all material risk data across the banking group

evidence_links.description: data_completeness term with BCBS 239 Principle 4 reference

principles.description: A bank should be able to generate aggregate risk data in a timely manner to meet all risk reporting requirements

evidence_links.description: data_timeliness term with BCBS 239 Principle 5 reference

principles.description: A bank should be able to generate aggregate risk data to meet a broad range of on-demand, ad hoc risk management reporting requests

evidence_links.description: 5 configurable detection models with JSON-driven parameters
concentration_risk term with BCBS 239 Principle 6 reference

principles.description: Risk management reports should accurately and precisely convey aggregated risk data and reflect risk in an exact manner

evidence_links.description: DAG-based calculation engine with topological sort, 4 layers (TRANSACTION→TIME_WINDOW→AGGREGATION→DERIVED)
Platinum tier (tier 6) for pre-built KPIs and executive dashboards

principles.gap_notes: Reporting accuracy validated through calculation DAG; no automated reconciliation with source systems in demo

principles.description: Risk management reports should cover all material risk areas within the organisation

evidence_links.description: 82 alerts across 5 models and 5 asset classes (equities, FX, futures, options, swaps)
Full data lineage for alert explainability

principles.gap_notes: Coverage is strong for market abuse; credit and operational risk models not in scope for demo

principles.description: Risk management reports should communicate information in a clear and concise manner

evidence_links.description: 45+ terms with ISO 11179 naming (Object Class + Property + Representation)
6 categories (market_abuse, data_entities, metrics, regulatory, data_quality, architecture)

principles.description: The board and senior management should set the frequency of risk management report production and distribution

evidence_links.description: Retention policies per tier (7 days to 7 years)

principles.gap_notes: Pipeline stages are defined but real-time scheduling engine is not implemented in demo; demo uses batch execution

principles.description: Risk management reports should be distributed to the relevant parties while ensuring confidentiality is maintained

evidence_links.description: 4 RBAC roles with tier-based and classification-based access control
5 masking algorithms (partial, tokenize, hash, generalize, redact) with role-based unmasking
Cross-view masking enforcement — role-based masking at response time for all data endpoints
PII registry endpoint — per-field masking status

principles.gap_notes: RBAC and masking enforced across all data endpoints; no external report distribution engine in demo
